NAME

client.c - 👥 Per-client lifecycle manager: threading coordination, state management, and client lifecycle orchestration

#define CLIENT_DISPATCH_HASH(type)

Variables


client_manager_t g_client_manager
Global client manager singleton - central coordination point. rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock = {0}
Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager.

Detailed Description

👥 Per-client lifecycle manager: threading coordination, state management, and client lifecycle orchestration

1. Client connection establishment and initializationinitializationPer-client thread creation and management (receive, send, render))Client state management with thread-safe access patternspatternsClient disconnection handling and resource cleanupcleanupHash table management for O(1) client lookupslookupsIntegration point between main.c and other modules THREADING ARCHITECTURE PER CLIENT:

Each connected client spawns multiple dedicated threads:

  • Receive Thread: Handles incoming packets from client (calls protocol.c functions)
  • Send Thread: Manages outgoing packet delivery using packet queues
  • Video Render Thread: Generates ASCII frames at 60fps (render.c)
  • Audio Render Thread: Mixes audio streams at 172fps (render.c)

This per-client threading model provides several advantages:

  • Linear performance scaling (no shared bottlenecks)
  • Fault isolation (one client's issues don't affect others)
  • Simplified synchronization (each client owns its resources)
  • Real-time performance guarantees per client

DATA STRUCTURES AND SYNCHRONIZATION:

The module manages two primary data structures:

1.
client_manager_t (global singleton):
•
Array of client_info_t structs (backing storage)
•
Hash table for O(1) client_id -> client_info_t lookups
•
Protected by reader-writer lock (g_client_manager_rwlock)
•
Allows concurrent reads, exclusive writes
2.
client_info_t (per-client state):
•
Network connection details and capabilities
•
Thread handles and synchronization primitives
•
Media buffers (video/audio) and packet queues
•
Terminal capabilities and rendering preferences
•
Protected by per-client mutex (client_state_mutex)

Synchronization Patterns:

LOCK ORDERING PROTOCOL (prevents deadlocks):

1.
Always acquire g_client_manager_rwlock FIRST
2.
Then acquire per-client mutexes if needed
3.
Release in reverse order

SNAPSHOT PATTERN (reduces lock contention):

1.
Acquire mutex
2.
Copy needed state variables to local copies
3.
Release mutex immediately
4.
Process using local copies without locks

INTEGRATION WITH OTHER MODULES:

  • main.c: Calls add_client() and remove_client() from main loop
  • protocol.c: Functions called by client receive threads for packet processing
  • render.c: Render thread functions created per client
  • stream.c: Stream generation functions called by render threads
  • stats.c: Accesses client data for performance monitoring

THREAD LIFECYCLE MANAGEMENT:

Thread creation order (in add_client()):

1.
Initialize client data structures and mutexes
2.
Create send thread (for outgoing packet delivery)
3.
Create receive thread (for incoming packet processing)
4.
Create render threads (video + audio generation)

Thread termination order (in remove_client()):

1.
Set shutdown flags (causes threads to exit main loops)
2.
Join send thread (cleanest exit, no blocking I/O)
3.
Join receive thread (may be blocked on network I/O)
4.
Join render threads (computational work, clean exit)
5.
Clean up resources (queues, buffers, mutexes)

WHY THIS MODULAR DESIGN:

The original server.c contained all client management code inline, making it:

  • Hard to understand the client lifecycle
  • Difficult to modify threading behavior
  • Impossible to isolate client-related bugs
  • Challenging to add new client features

This modular approach provides:

  • Clear separation of client vs. server concerns
  • Easier testing of client management logic
  • Better code reuse and maintenance
  • Future extensibility for new client types

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Version

2.0 (Post-Modularization)

See also

main.c For overall server architecture

render.c For per-client rendering implementation

protocol.c For client packet processing

613                                                                                                           {
614   log_info("[TCP_ADD_CLIENT] ENTER: client_ip=%s, port=%d", client_ip, port);
615   // Find empty slot WITHOUT holding the global lock
616   // We'll re-verify under lock after allocations complete
617   int slot = -1;
618   int existing_count = 0;
619   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
620     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
621       slot = i; // Take first available slot
622     }
623     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
624       existing_count++;
625     }
626   }
627 
628   // Quick pre-check before expensive allocations
629   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_CHECK: existing_count=%d, max_clients=%d, slot=%d", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients),
630            slot);
631   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients) || slot == -1) {
632     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Maximum client limit reached\n";
633     ssize_t send_result = socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
634     if (send_result < 0) {
635       log_warn("Failed to send rejection message to client: %s", SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
636     }
637     return NULL;
638   }
639 
640   // NOW acquire the lock early for generating unique client name
641   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRE_START");
642   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
643   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED");
644 
645   // Generate unique client ID (noun only, without counter or port)
646   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
647   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_START");
648   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
649     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
650     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID");
651     return NULL;
652   }
653   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_DONE: id=%s", new_client_id);
654 
655   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASE_START");
656   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
657   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASED");
658 
659   // DO EXPENSIVE ALLOCATIONS OUTSIDE THE LOCK
660   // This prevents blocking frame processing during client initialization
661   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_START: About to create video frame buffer for client_id=%s", new_client_id);
662   video_frame_buffer_t *incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
663   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_DONE: Video buffer creation returned %p", (void *)incoming_video_buffer);
664   if (!incoming_video_buffer) {
665     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
666     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
667     return NULL;
668   }
669 
670   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_DONE: About to create audio buffer");
671   audio_ring_buffer_t *incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
672   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_DONE: Audio buffer created");
673   if (!incoming_audio_buffer) {
674     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
675     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
676     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
677     return NULL;
678   }
679 
680   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_START: Creating audio queue");
681   packet_queue_t *audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
682   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_DONE");
683   if (!audio_queue) {
684     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for client");
685     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
686     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
687     return NULL;
688   }
689 
690   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_START: Creating outgoing video buffer");
691   video_frame_buffer_t *outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
692   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_DONE");
693   if (!outgoing_video_buffer) {
694     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for client");
695     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
696     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
697     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
698     return NULL;
699   }
700 
701   void *send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, 64, void *);
702   if (!send_buffer) {
703     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
704     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
705     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
706     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
707     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
708     return NULL;
709   }
710 
711   // NOW acquire the lock for the critical section: slot assignment + registration
712   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_START: About to acquire client manager write lock");
713   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
714   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED: Client manager lock acquired");
715   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_RECHECK: About to check if slot is still available");
716 
717   // Re-check slot availability under lock (another thread might have taken it)
718   if (g_client_manager.clients[slot].client_id[0] != '\0') {
719     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
720     SAFE_FREE(send_buffer);
721     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
722     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
723     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
724     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
725 
726     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Slot reassigned, try again\n";
727     socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
728     return NULL;
729   }
730 
731   // Now we have exclusive access to the slot - do the actual registration
732   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
733   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
734 
735   // Initialize codec capabilities to zero (no codecs supported until CLIENT_CAPABILITIES received)
736   client->codec_capabilities_video = 0;
737   client->codec_capabilities_audio = 0;
738 
739   client->socket = socket;
740   client->is_tcp_client = true;
741   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
742   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
743   client->port = port;
744   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
745   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for cleanup
746   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
747   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0);
748   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);
749   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
750 
751   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
752   client->crypto_initialized = false;
753 
754   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
755   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
756   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
757 
758   // Assign pre-allocated buffers
759   client->incoming_video_buffer = incoming_video_buffer;
760   client->incoming_audio_buffer = incoming_audio_buffer;
761   client->audio_queue = audio_queue;
762   client->outgoing_video_buffer = outgoing_video_buffer;
763   client->send_buffer = send_buffer;
764   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
765 
766   // Generate unique noun-based client name with transport type and port
767   // Note: We're holding the write lock here, so it's safe to iterate existing clients
768   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME");
769   if (generate_client_name(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), g_client_manager.clients_by_id, port,
770                            true /* is_tcp */) != 0) {
771     // Fallback to numeric name if generation fails
772     safe_snprintf(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), "client_%u (tcp:%d)", new_client_id, port);
773   }
774   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME: display_name=%s", client->display_name);
775 
776   // Register client with named debug system using the generated name
777   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT(client, client->display_name, NULL);
778 
779   log_info("Added new client %s from %s:%d (socket=%d, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, port, socket, slot);
780   log_debug("Client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d, socket=%d", new_client_id, slot, socket);
781 
782   // Register socket with tcp_server
783   asciichat_error_t reg_result = tcp_server_add_client(server_ctx->tcp_server, socket, client);
784   if (reg_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
785     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to register client socket with tcp_server");
786     log_error("Failed to register client %s socket with tcp_server", new_client_id);
787     // Don't unlock here - error_cleanup will do it
788     goto error_cleanup;
789   }
790 
791   g_client_manager.client_count++;
792   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added client_id=%s to slot %d)", g_client_manager.client_count,
793             new_client_id, slot);
794 
795   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
796   log_debug("Added client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
797 
798   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
799 
800   // Configure socket OUTSIDE lock
801   configure_client_socket(socket, new_client_id);
802 
803   // Initialize mutexes OUTSIDE lock BEFORE registering them
804   // register_client_info_atomics needs these mutexes to be initialized first
805   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
806     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
807     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
808     return NULL;
809   }
810 
811   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
812     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
813     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
814     return NULL;
815   }
816 
817   // Register all atomic fields for debug tracking - DISABLED to prevent deadlock
818   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() call
819   // NAMED_REGISTER which tries to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock
820   // The debug_sync thread holds READ lock while printing, causing reader-writer deadlock
821   // These are debug-only features not critical for functionality
822   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
823   // register_client_info_atomics(client);
824   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
825 
826   // Audio buffer atomics registration disabled - same deadlock reason
827   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_START: client=%p", (void *)client);
828   // if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
829   //   audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics(client->incoming_audio_buffer, new_client_id);
830   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_DONE");
831   // } else {
832   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_SKIPPED: incoming_audio_buffer is NULL");
833   // }
834 
835   // Register with audio mixer OUTSIDE lock
836   // CRITICAL: Do this BEFORE crypto handshake to avoid deadlock with mixer thread
837   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_START: g_audio_mixer=%p, client->incoming_audio_buffer=%p", (void *)g_audio_mixer,
838            (void *)client->incoming_audio_buffer);
839   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
840     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_CALLING: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
841     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
842       log_warn("Failed to add client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
843     } else {
844       log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SUCCESS: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
845 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
846       log_debug("Added client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
847 #endif
848     }
849   } else {
850     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SKIPPED: conditions not met");
851   }
852   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_DONE");
853 
854   // Perform crypto handshake before starting threads.
855   // This ensures the handshake uses the socket directly without interference from receive thread.
856   log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_START: About to call server_crypto_init()");
857   if (server_crypto_init() == 0) {
858     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_DONE: server_crypto_init() returned 0");
859     // Set timeout for crypto handshake to prevent indefinite blocking
860     // This prevents clients from connecting but never completing the handshake
861     const uint64_t HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS = 30ULL * NS_PER_SEC_INT;
862     asciichat_error_t timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS);
863     if (timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
864       log_warn("Failed to set handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
865                asciichat_error_string(timeout_result));
866       // Continue anyway - timeout is a safety feature, not critical
867     }
868 
869     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_START: About to call server_crypto_handshake()");
870     int crypto_result = server_crypto_handshake(client);
871     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_DONE: result=%d", crypto_result);
872     if (crypto_result != 0) {
873       log_error("Crypto handshake failed for client %s: %s", new_client_id, network_error_string());
874       if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
875         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
876       }
877       return NULL;
878     }
879 
880     // Clear socket timeout after handshake completes successfully
881     // This allows normal operation without timeouts on data transfer
882     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CLEAR_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_START");
883     asciichat_error_t clear_timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, 0);
884     if (clear_timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
885       log_warn("Failed to clear handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
886                asciichat_error_string(clear_timeout_result));
887       // Continue anyway - we can still communicate even with timeout set
888     }
889 
890     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s", new_client_id);
891 
892     // Create ACIP transport for protocol-agnostic packet sending
893     // The transport wraps the socket with encryption context from the handshake
894     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_server_get_context(new_client_id);
895     client->transport = acip_tcp_transport_create(new_client_id, socket, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
896     if (!client->transport) {
897       log_error("Failed to create ACIP transport for client %s", new_client_id);
898       if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
899         log_error("Failed to remove client after transport creation failure");
900       }
901       return NULL;
902     }
903     log_debug("Created ACIP transport for client %s with crypto context", new_client_id);
904 
905     // After handshake completes, the client immediately sends PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES
906     // We must read and process this packet BEFORE starting the receive thread to avoid a race condition
907     // where the packet arrives but no thread is listening for it.
908     //
909     // SPECIAL CASE: If client used --no-encrypt, we already received this packet during the handshake
910     // attempt and stored it in pending_packet_*. Use that instead of receiving a new one.
911     packet_envelope_t envelope;
912     bool used_pending_packet = false;
913 
914     if (client->pending_packet_payload) {
915       // Client used --no-encrypt mode - use the packet we already received
916       log_info("Client %s using --no-encrypt mode - processing pending packet type %u", client->client_id,
917                client->pending_packet_type);
918       envelope.type = client->pending_packet_type;
919       envelope.data = client->pending_packet_payload;
920       envelope.len = client->pending_packet_length;
921       envelope.allocated_buffer = client->pending_packet_payload; // Will be freed below
922       envelope.allocated_size = client->pending_packet_length;
923       used_pending_packet = true;
924 
925       // Clear pending packet fields
926       client->pending_packet_type = 0;
927       client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
928       client->pending_packet_length = 0;
929     } else {
930       // Normal encrypted mode - receive capabilities packet
931       log_debug("Waiting for initial capabilities packet from client %s", client->client_id);
932 
933       // Protect crypto context access with client state mutex
934       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
935       const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_server_get_context(client->client_id);
936 
937       // Use per-client crypto state to determine enforcement
938       // At this point, handshake is complete, so crypto_initialized=true and handshake is ready
939       bool enforce_encryption = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
940                                 crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
941 
942       packet_recv_result_t result = receive_packet_secure(socket, (void *)crypto_ctx, enforce_encryption, &envelope);
943       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
944 
945       if (result != PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS) {
946         log_error("Failed to receive initial capabilities packet from client %s: result=%d", client->client_id, result);
947         if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
948           buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
949         }
950         if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
951           log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
952         }
953         return NULL;
954       }
955     }
956 
957     if (envelope.type != PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES) {
958       log_error("Expected PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES but got packet type %d from client %s", envelope.type,
959                 client->client_id);
960       if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
961         buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
962       }
963       if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
964         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
965       }
966       return NULL;
967     }
968 
969     // Process the capabilities packet directly
970     log_debug("Processing initial capabilities packet from client %s (from %s)", client->client_id,
971               used_pending_packet ? "pending packet" : "network");
972     handle_client_capabilities_packet(client, envelope.data, envelope.len);
973 
974     // Free the packet data
975     if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
976       buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
977     }
978     log_debug("Successfully received and processed initial capabilities for client %s", client->client_id);
979   }
980 
981   // Start all client threads in the correct order (unified path for TCP and WebRTC)
982   // This creates: receive thread -> render threads -> send thread
983   // The render threads MUST be created before send thread to avoid the race condition
984   // where send thread reads empty frames before render thread generates the first real frame
985   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
986   if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, true) != 0) {
987     log_error("Failed to start threads for TCP client %s", client_id_snapshot);
988     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
989     remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot);
990     return NULL;
991   }
992   log_debug("Successfully created render threads for client %s", client_id_snapshot);
993 
994   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
995   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
996     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, socket, client_ip, port);
997     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
998       log_warn("Failed to register client %s with session_host", client_id_snapshot);
999     } else {
1000       log_debug("Client %s registered with session_host as %u", client_id_snapshot, client->session_client_id);
1001     }
1002   }
1003 
1004   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1005   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1006   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_START: About to broadcast to all clients");
1007   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1008   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_DONE");
1009 
1010   log_info("[TCP_DBG] ADD_CLIENT_RETURNING: client_id=%s, client=%p", client->client_id, (void *)client);
1011   return client;
1012 
1013 error_cleanup:
1014   // Clean up all partially allocated resources
1015   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1016   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1017   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1018   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1019   return NULL;
1020 }

client_info_t * add_webrtc_client (server_context_t
  * server_ctx, acip_transport_t * transport, const char * client_ip,
  bool start_threads)


Register a WebRTC client with the server. Registers a client that
    connected via WebRTC data channel instead of TCP socket. This function
    reuses most of add_client() logic but skips:


    
  
  • Crypto handshake (already done via ACDS signaling)
    • 
  
  • Socket-specific configuration
    • 
  
  • TCP thread pool registration
    • 



DIFFERENCES FROM add_client():


    
  
  • Takes an already-created acip_transport_t* instead of socket
    • 
  
  • No crypto handshake (WebRTC signaling handled authentication)
    • 
  
  • No socket configuration (WebRTC handles buffering)
    • 
  
  • Uses generic thread spawning instead of tcp_server thread pool
    • 



Parameters


server_ctx Server context


transport WebRTC transport (already created and connected)


client_ip Client IP address for logging (may be empty for P2P)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t on success, NULL on
  failure


Note


The transport must be fully initialized and ready to
  send/receive



Client capabilities are still expected as first packet




Definition at line 1045 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1046                                                      {
1047   if (!server_ctx || !transport || !client_ip) {
1048     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters to add_webrtc_client");
1049     return NULL;
1050   }
1051 
1052   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1053 
1054   // Find empty slot - this is the authoritative check
1055   int slot = -1;
1056   int existing_count = 0;
1057   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1058     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
1059       slot = i; // Take first available slot
1060     }
1061     // Count only active clients
1062     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
1063       existing_count++;
1064     }
1065   }
1066 
1067   // Check if we've hit the configured max-clients limit (not the array size)
1068   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients)) {
1069     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1070     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count,
1071               GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1072     log_error("Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1073     return NULL;
1074   }
1075 
1076   if (slot == -1) {
1077     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1078     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1079     log_error("No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1080     return NULL;
1081   }
1082 
1083   // Update client_count to match actual count before adding new client
1084   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count;
1085 
1086   // Generate unique client ID for WebRTC client (noun only, without counter or port)
1087   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
1088   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
1089     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1090     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID for WebRTC client");
1091     return NULL;
1092   }
1093 
1094   // Initialize client
1095   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
1096 
1097   // Free any existing buffers from previous client in this slot
1098   if (client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1099     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1100     client->incoming_video_buffer = NULL;
1101   }
1102   if (client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1103     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
1104     client->outgoing_video_buffer = NULL;
1105   }
1106   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1107     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_audio_buffer);
1108     client->incoming_audio_buffer = NULL;
1109   }
1110   if (client->send_buffer) {
1111     SAFE_FREE(client->send_buffer);
1112     client->send_buffer = NULL;
1113   }
1114 
1115   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1116 
1117   // Set up WebRTC-specific fields
1118   client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // WebRTC has no traditional socket
1119   client->is_tcp_client = false;         // WebRTC client - threads managed directly
1120   client->transport = transport;         // Use provided transport
1121   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
1122   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
1123   client->port = 0; // WebRTC doesn't use port numbers
1124   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
1125   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for receive thread cleanup
1126   log_info("Added new WebRTC client %s from %s (transport=%p, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, transport, slot);
1127   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
1128   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0); // Render thread updates this
1129   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);     // Send thread updates this
1130   log_debug("WebRTC client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d", client->client_id, slot);
1131   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
1132 
1133   // Initialize crypto context for this client
1134   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
1135   log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Setting crypto_initialized=false (will be set to true after handshake completes)");
1136   // Do NOT set crypto_initialized = true here! The handshake must complete first.
1137   // For WebSocket clients: websocket_client_handler will perform the handshake
1138   // For WebRTC clients: ACDS signaling may have already done crypto (future enhancement)
1139   client->crypto_initialized = false;
1140 
1141   // Initialize pending packet storage (unused for WebRTC, but keep for consistency)
1142   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
1143   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
1144   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
1145 
1146   // Use noun-based client name for display_name as well
1147   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->display_name, new_client_id, sizeof(client->display_name) - 1);
1148 
1149   // Create individual video buffer for this client using modern double-buffering
1150   client->incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1151   if (!client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1152     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1153     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1154     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1155   }
1156 
1157   // Create individual audio buffer for this client
1158   client->incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
1159   if (!client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1160     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1161     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1162     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1163   }
1164 
1165   // Create packet queues for outgoing data
1166   client->audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
1167   if (!client->audio_queue) {
1168     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for WebRTC client");
1169     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1170   }
1171 
1172   // Create packet queue for async dispatch: received packets waiting to be processed
1173   // Capacity of 100 packets allows buffering of ~10-50 frames (depending on fragmentation)
1174   client->received_packet_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(100, 500, false);
1175   if (!client->received_packet_queue) {
1176     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create received packet queue for client");
1177     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1178   }
1179 
1180   // Create outgoing video buffer for ASCII frames (double buffered, no dropping)
1181   client->outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1182   if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1183     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for WebRTC client");
1184     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1185   }
1186 
1187   // Pre-allocate send buffer to avoid malloc/free in send thread (prevents deadlocks)
1188   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE; // 2MB should handle largest frames
1189   client->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(client->send_buffer_size, 64, void *);
1190   if (!client->send_buffer) {
1191     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1192     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1193   }
1194 
1195   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count + 1; // We just added a client
1196   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added WebRTC client_id=%s to slot %d)",
1197             g_client_manager.client_count, new_client_id, slot);
1198 
1199   // Add client to uthash table for O(1) lookup
1200   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
1201   log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
1202 
1203   // Release the write lock IMMEDIATELY after adding to hash table
1204   // All subsequent operations (mutex init, mixer registration) don't need global client manager lock
1205   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1206 
1207   // Register this client's audio buffer with the mixer
1208   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1209     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
1210       log_warn("Failed to add WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1211     } else {
1212 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1213       log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1214 #endif
1215     }
1216   }
1217 
1218   // Initialize mutexes BEFORE creating any threads to prevent race conditions
1219   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
1220     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1221     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1222     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
1223     return NULL;
1224   }
1225 
1226   // Initialize send mutex to protect concurrent socket writes
1227   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
1228     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1229     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1230     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1231     return NULL;
1232   }
1233 
1234   // Initialize condition variable for dispatch thread wake-up
1235   if (cond_init(&client->dispatch_queue_cond, "dispatch_queue_cond") != 0) {
1236     log_error("Failed to initialize dispatch queue condition variable for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1237     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1238     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1239     return NULL;
1240   }
1241 
1242   // For WebRTC clients, the capabilities packet will be received by the receive thread
1243   // when it starts. Unlike TCP clients where we handle it synchronously in add_client(),
1244   // WebRTC uses the transport abstraction which handles packet reception automatically.
1245   log_debug("WebRTC client %s initialized - receive thread will process capabilities", new_client_id);
1246 
1247   // Conditionally start threads based on caller preference
1248   // WebSocket handler passes start_threads=false to defer thread startup until after crypto init
1249   // This ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto context is ready
1250   if (start_threads) {
1251     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Starting client threads (receive, render) for client %s...", new_client_id);
1252     if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false) != 0) {
1253       log_error("Failed to start threads for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1254       return NULL;
1255     }
1256     log_debug("Created receive thread for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1257     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Receive thread started - thread will now be processing packets", new_client_id);
1258   } else {
1259     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Deferring thread startup for client %s (caller will start after crypto init)",
1260               new_client_id);
1261   }
1262 
1263   // Send initial server state to the new client
1264   if (send_server_state_to_client(client) != 0) {
1265     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1266   } else {
1267 #ifdef DEBUG_NETWORK
1268     log_info("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1269 #endif
1270   }
1271 
1272   // Send initial server state via ACIP transport
1273   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1274   uint32_t connected_count = g_client_manager.client_count;
1275   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1276 
1277   server_state_packet_t state;
1278   state.connected_client_count = connected_count;
1279   state.active_client_count = 0; // Will be updated by broadcast thread
1280   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
1281 
1282   // Convert to network byte order
1283   server_state_packet_t net_state;
1284   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
1285   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
1286   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
1287 
1288   asciichat_error_t packet_send_result = acip_send_server_state(client->transport, &net_state);
1289   if (packet_send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1290     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %s", new_client_id,
1291              asciichat_error_string(packet_send_result));
1292   } else {
1293     log_debug("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %u connected clients", new_client_id,
1294               state.connected_client_count);
1295   }
1296 
1297   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
1298   // WebRTC clients use INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE since they don't have a TCP socket
1299   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
1300     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, client_ip, 0);
1301     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
1302       log_warn("Failed to register WebRTC client %s with session_host", new_client_id);
1303     } else {
1304       log_debug("WebRTC client %s registered with session_host as %u", new_client_id, client->session_client_id);
1305     }
1306   }
1307 
1308   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1309   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1310   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1311 
1312   return client;
1313 
1314 error_cleanup_webrtc:
1315   // Clean up all partially allocated resources for WebRTC client
1316   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1317   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1318   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1319   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1320   return NULL;
1321 }

void
  broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients (void )


Notify all clients of state changes.


Definition at line 2527 of file
  src/server/client.c.


2527                                                  {
2528   // SNAPSHOT PATTERN: Collect client data while holding lock, then release before network I/O
2529   typedef struct {
2530     socket_t socket;
2531     char client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
2532     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx;
2533   } client_snapshot_t;
2534 
2535   client_snapshot_t client_snapshots[MAX_CLIENTS];
2536   int snapshot_count = 0;
2537   int active_video_count = 0;
2538 
2539   uint64_t lock_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2540   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2541   uint64_t lock_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2542   uint64_t lock_time_ns = time_elapsed_ns(lock_start_ns, lock_end_ns);
2543   if (lock_time_ns > 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT) {
2544     char duration_str[32];
2545     time_pretty((uint64_t)((double)lock_time_ns), -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
2546     log_warn("broadcast_server_state: rwlock_rdlock took %s", duration_str);
2547   }
2548 
2549   // Count active clients and snapshot client data while holding lock
2550   // Use atomic_load for all atomic fields to prevent data races.
2551   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2552     bool is_active = atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active);
2553     if (is_active && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].is_sending_video)) {
2554       active_video_count++;
2555     }
2556     if (is_active && g_client_manager.clients[i].socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2557       // Skip clients that haven't completed crypto handshake yet
2558       // Check both crypto_initialized AND crypto context (defense in depth)
2559       if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && !g_client_manager.clients[i].crypto_initialized) {
2560         log_debug("Skipping server_state broadcast to client %s: crypto handshake not complete",
2561                   g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id);
2562         continue;
2563       }
2564 
2565       // Get crypto context (may be NULL if --no-encrypt)
2566       const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx =
2567           crypto_handshake_get_context(&g_client_manager.clients[i].crypto_handshake_ctx);
2568       if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && !crypto_ctx) {
2569         // Skip clients without valid crypto context
2570         log_debug("Skipping server_state broadcast to client %s: no crypto context",
2571                   g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id);
2572         continue;
2573       }
2574 
2575       client_snapshots[snapshot_count].socket = g_client_manager.clients[i].socket;
2576       SAFE_STRNCPY(client_snapshots[snapshot_count].client_id, g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id,
2577                    sizeof(client_snapshots[snapshot_count].client_id) - 1);
2578       client_snapshots[snapshot_count].crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
2579       snapshot_count++;
2580     }
2581   }
2582 
2583   // Prepare server state packet while still holding lock (fast operation)
2584   server_state_packet_t state;
2585   state.connected_client_count = g_client_manager.client_count;
2586   state.active_client_count = active_video_count;
2587   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
2588 
2589   // Convert to network byte order
2590   server_state_packet_t net_state;
2591   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
2592   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
2593   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
2594 
2595   // Release lock BEFORE sending (snapshot pattern)
2596   // Sending while holding lock blocks all client operations
2597   uint64_t lock_held_final_ns = time_get_ns();
2598   uint64_t lock_held_ns = time_elapsed_ns(lock_start_ns, lock_held_final_ns);
2599   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2600 
2601   // Send to all clients AFTER releasing the lock
2602   // This prevents blocking other threads during network I/O
2603   for (int i = 0; i < snapshot_count; i++) {
2604     log_debug_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2605                     "BROADCAST_DEBUG: Sending SERVER_STATE to client %s (socket %d) with crypto_ctx=%p",
2606                     client_snapshots[i].client_id, client_snapshots[i].socket, (void *)client_snapshots[i].crypto_ctx);
2607 
2608     // Protect socket write with per-client send_mutex.
2609     client_info_t *target = find_client_by_id(client_snapshots[i].client_id);
2610     if (target) {
2611       // IMPORTANT: Verify client_id matches expected value - prevents use-after-free
2612       // if client was removed and replaced with another client in same slot
2613       if (strcmp(target->client_id, client_snapshots[i].client_id) != 0) {
2614         log_warn("Client %s ID mismatch during broadcast (found %s), skipping send", client_snapshots[i].client_id,
2615                  target->client_id);
2616         continue;
2617       }
2618 
2619       mutex_lock(&target->send_mutex);
2620 
2621       // Double-check client_id again after acquiring mutex (stronger protection)
2622       if (strcmp(target->client_id, client_snapshots[i].client_id) != 0) {
2623         mutex_unlock(&target->send_mutex);
2624         log_warn("Client %s was removed during broadcast send (now %s), skipping", client_snapshots[i].client_id,
2625                  target->client_id);
2626         continue;
2627       }
2628 
2629       // Send via ACIP transport
2630       asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_server_state(target->transport, &net_state);
2631       mutex_unlock(&target->send_mutex);
2632 
2633       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2634         log_error("Failed to send server state to client %s: %s", client_snapshots[i].client_id,
2635                   asciichat_error_string(result));
2636       } else {
2637         log_debug_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Sent server state to client %s: %u connected, %u active",
2638                         client_snapshots[i].client_id, state.connected_client_count, state.active_client_count);
2639       }
2640     } else {
2641       log_warn("Client %s removed before broadcast send could complete", client_snapshots[i].client_id);
2642     }
2643   }
2644 
2645   if (lock_held_ns > 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT) {
2646     char duration_str[32];
2647     time_pretty((uint64_t)((double)lock_held_ns), -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
2648     log_warn("broadcast_server_state: rwlock held for %s (includes network I/O)", duration_str);
2649   }
2650 }

void
  * client_dispatch_thread (void * arg)


Async dispatch thread for WebRTC clients. Async dispatch thread -
    processes queued received packets.




Decouples receive from dispatch to prevent backpressure on the
    network socket. Received packets are queued by the receive thread, and
    processed asynchronously here. This allows the receive thread to keep
    accepting packets while dispatch processes them.


Definition at line 1628 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1628                                         {
1629   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1630 
1631   if (!client) {
1632     log_error("Invalid client info in dispatch thread (NULL pointer)");
1633     return NULL;
1634   }
1635 
1636   const char *client_id = client->client_id;
1637   log_info("DISPATCH_THREAD: Started for client %s", client_id);
1638 
1639   uint64_t dispatch_loop_count = 0;
1640 
1641   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && atomic_load_bool(&client->dispatch_thread_running)) {
1642     dispatch_loop_count++;
1643     // Try to dequeue next packet (non-blocking)
1644     // Use try_dequeue to avoid blocking - allows checking exit flag frequently
1645     uint64_t dequeue_start = time_get_ns();
1646     queued_packet_t *queued_pkt = packet_queue_try_dequeue(client->received_packet_queue);
1647     uint64_t dequeue_end = time_get_ns();
1648 
1649     if (!queued_pkt) {
1650       // Queue was empty, wait for signal from receive thread or timeout
1651       // Use 100ms timeout to handle cases where signal is missed due to race conditions
1652       log_dev_every(5 * US_PER_MS_INT, "🔄 DISPATCH_LOOP[%llu]: Queue empty after %.1fμs, waiting for signal",
1653                     (unsigned long long)dispatch_loop_count, (dequeue_end - dequeue_start) / 1000.0);
1654       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1655       cond_timedwait(&client->dispatch_queue_cond, &client->client_state_mutex, 100 * 1000 * 1000); // 100ms timeout
1656       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1657       continue;
1658     }
1659 
1660     // Frame received! Log it immediately
1661     char dequeue_elapsed_str[32];
1662     time_pretty(dequeue_end - dequeue_start, -1, dequeue_elapsed_str, sizeof(dequeue_elapsed_str));
1663     log_info("✅ DISPATCH_THREAD[%u] DEQUEUED packet: %zu bytes (dequeue took %s)", client_id, queued_pkt->data_len,
1664              dequeue_elapsed_str);
1665 
1666     // Process the dequeued packet
1667     // The queued packet contains the complete ACIP packet (header + payload) from websocket_recv()
1668     const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)queued_pkt->data;
1669     size_t total_len = queued_pkt->data_len;
1670     uint8_t *payload = (uint8_t *)header + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1671     size_t payload_len = 0;
1672 
1673     log_info("🔍 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Processing %zu byte packet (header size=%zu)", client_id, total_len,
1674              sizeof(packet_header_t));
1675 
1676     if (total_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1677       log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Packet too small (%zu < %zu), DROPPING", client_id, total_len,
1678                 sizeof(packet_header_t));
1679       packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1680       continue;
1681     }
1682 
1683     {
1684       packet_type_t packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
1685       payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
1686 
1687       log_info("🎯 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Packet type=%d, payload_len=%u, total_len=%zu", client_id, packet_type,
1688                payload_len, total_len);
1689 
1690       // Handle PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED from WebSocket clients that encrypt at application layer
1691       // This mirrors the decryption logic in acip_server_receive_and_dispatch()
1692       if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED && client->transport && client->transport->crypto_ctx) {
1693         log_info("🔐 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Decrypting PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED", client_id);
1694 
1695         uint8_t *ciphertext = payload;
1696         size_t ciphertext_len = payload_len;
1697 
1698         // Decrypt to get inner plaintext packet (header + payload)
1699         size_t plaintext_size = ciphertext_len + 1024;
1700         uint8_t *plaintext = SAFE_MALLOC(plaintext_size, uint8_t *);
1701         if (!plaintext) {
1702           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Failed to allocate plaintext buffer for decryption", client_id);
1703           packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1704           continue;
1705         }
1706 
1707         size_t plaintext_len;
1708         crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_decrypt(client->transport->crypto_ctx, ciphertext, ciphertext_len,
1709                                                        plaintext, plaintext_size, &plaintext_len);
1710 
1711         if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
1712           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Failed to decrypt packet: %s", client_id,
1713                     crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
1714           SAFE_FREE(plaintext);
1715           packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1716           continue;
1717         }
1718 
1719         if (plaintext_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1720           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Decrypted packet too small: %zu < %zu", client_id, plaintext_len,
1721                     sizeof(packet_header_t));
1722           SAFE_FREE(plaintext);
1723           packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1724           continue;
1725         }
1726 
1727         // Parse the inner (decrypted) header
1728         const packet_header_t *inner_header = (const packet_header_t *)plaintext;
1729         packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(inner_header->type);
1730         payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(inner_header->length);
1731         payload = plaintext + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1732 
1733         log_info("🔐 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Decrypted inner packet type=%d, payload_len=%u", client_id, packet_type,
1734                  payload_len);
1735 
1736         // Dispatch the decrypted packet
1737         if (client->transport) {
1738           log_info("🎯 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Calling acip_handle_server_packet(type=%d, payload_len=%u)", client_id,
1739                    packet_type, payload_len);
1740           asciichat_error_t dispatch_result = acip_handle_server_packet(client->transport, packet_type, payload,
1741                                                                         payload_len, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1742 
1743           if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1744             log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Handler failed for decrypted packet type=%d: %s", client_id, packet_type,
1745                       asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1746           } else {
1747             log_info("✅ DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Successfully dispatched decrypted packet type=%d", client_id,
1748                      packet_type);
1749           }
1750         } else {
1751           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Cannot dispatch decrypted packet - transport is NULL", client_id);
1752         }
1753 
1754         // Free the decrypted buffer
1755         SAFE_FREE(plaintext);
1756       } else {
1757         // Not encrypted or no crypto context - dispatch as-is
1758         if (client->transport) {
1759           log_info("🎯 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Calling acip_handle_server_packet(type=%d, payload_len=%u)", client_id,
1760                    packet_type, payload_len);
1761           asciichat_error_t dispatch_result = acip_handle_server_packet(client->transport, packet_type, payload,
1762                                                                         payload_len, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1763 
1764           if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1765             log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Handler failed for packet type=%d: %s", client_id, packet_type,
1766                       asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1767           } else {
1768             log_info("✅ DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Successfully dispatched packet type=%d", client_id, packet_type);
1769           }
1770         } else {
1771           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Cannot dispatch packet - transport is NULL", client_id);
1772         }
1773       }
1774     }
1775 
1776     // Free the queued packet
1777     packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1778   }
1779 
1780   log_info("DISPATCH_THREAD: Exiting for client %s", client_id);
1781   return NULL;
1782 }

void
  * client_receive_thread (void * arg)


Definition at line 1784 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1784                                        {
1785   // Log thread startup
1786   log_debug("RECV_THREAD: Thread function entered, arg=%p", arg);
1787 
1788   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1789 
1790   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.
1791   // This prevents crashes if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct
1792   // while the thread was still starting at RtlUserThreadStart.
1793   if (!client) {
1794     log_error("Invalid client info in receive thread (NULL pointer)");
1795     return NULL;
1796   }
1797 
1798   // Save this thread's ID so remove_client() can detect self-joins
1799   client->receive_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1800 
1801   log_debug("RECV_THREAD_DEBUG: Thread started, client=%p, client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d", (void *)client, client->client_id,
1802             client->is_tcp_client);
1803 
1804   if (atomic_load_bool(&client->protocol_disconnect_requested)) {
1805     log_debug("Receive thread for client %s exiting before start (protocol disconnect requested)", client->client_id);
1806     return NULL;
1807   }
1808 
1809   // Check if client_id is empty (client struct has been zeroed by remove_client)
1810   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
1811   if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1812     log_debug("Receive thread: client_id is empty, client struct may have been zeroed, exiting");
1813     return NULL;
1814   }
1815 
1816   // Additional validation: check socket is valid
1817   // For TCP clients, validate socket. WebRTC clients use DataChannel (no socket)
1818   if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1819     log_error("Invalid client socket in receive thread");
1820     return NULL;
1821   }
1822 
1823   // NOTE: Atomic registration disabled
1824   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() attempt
1825   // to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock, which conflicts with debug_sync
1826   // thread holding READ lock during printing, causing deadlock.
1827   // These are debug-only features, not critical for functionality.
1828   // The atomics work fine without being registered in the debug system.
1829 
1830   // Enable thread cancellation for clean shutdown
1831   // Thread cancellation not available in platform abstraction
1832   // Threads should exit when g_should_exit is set
1833 
1834   log_debug("Started receive thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
1835 
1836   // Main receive loop - processes packets from transport
1837   // For TCP clients: receives from socket
1838   // For WebRTC clients: receives from transport ringbuffer (via ACDS signaling)
1839 
1840   log_info("RECV_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d, transport=%p, active=%d", client->client_id,
1841            client->is_tcp_client, (void *)client->transport, atomic_load_bool(&client->active));
1842 
1843   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && atomic_load_bool(&client->active)) {
1844     // For TCP clients, check socket validity
1845     // For WebRTC clients, continue even if no socket (transport handles everything)
1846     if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1847       log_debug("TCP client %s has invalid socket, exiting receive thread", client->client_id);
1848       break;
1849     }
1850 
1851     // Check client_id is still valid before accessing transport.
1852     // This prevents accessing freed memory if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct.
1853     if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1854       log_debug("Client client_id reset, exiting receive thread");
1855       break;
1856     }
1857 
1858     // Receive packet (without dispatching) - decouple from dispatch for async processing
1859     // For WebRTC clients with async dispatch, we queue packets instead of processing immediately
1860     // This prevents backpressure on the network socket
1861 
1862     if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1863       // TCP clients: use original synchronous dispatch
1864       asciichat_error_t acip_result =
1865           acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(client->transport, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1866 
1867       // Check if shutdown was requested during the network call
1868       if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
1869         log_debug("RECV_EXIT: Server shutdown requested, breaking loop");
1870         break;
1871       }
1872 
1873       // Handle receive errors
1874       if (acip_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1875         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1876         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1877           log_error("🔴 ACIP error for client %s: code=%u msg=%s", client->client_id, err_ctx.code,
1878                     err_ctx.context_message);
1879           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1880             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1881             break;
1882           } else if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_CRYPTO) {
1883             log_error_client(
1884                 client, "SECURITY VIOLATION: Unencrypted packet when encryption required - terminating connection");
1885             atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1886             break;
1887           }
1888         }
1889         log_warn("ACIP error for TCP client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1890                  asciichat_error_string(acip_result));
1891         break;
1892       }
1893     } else {
1894       // WebRTC/WebSocket clients: async dispatch - receive packet and queue for async processing
1895       void *packet_data = NULL;
1896       void *allocated_buffer = NULL;
1897       size_t packet_len = 0;
1898 
1899       // Snapshot transport pointer to prevent use-after-free
1900       // Transport can be destroyed by WebSocket callback while we're receiving
1901       acip_transport_t *transport_snapshot = NULL;
1902       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
1903       if (!client->transport || atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1904         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1905         log_debug("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Transport destroyed or client shutting down, exiting receive loop",
1906                   client->client_id);
1907         break;
1908       }
1909       transport_snapshot = client->transport;
1910       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1911 
1912       log_debug("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: About to call transport->recv() (transport=%p)", client->client_id,
1913                 (void *)transport_snapshot);
1914 
1915       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1916           transport_snapshot->methods->recv(transport_snapshot, &packet_data, &packet_len, &allocated_buffer);
1917 
1918       // Check error BEFORE logging - logging system may clear thread-local errno
1919       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1920         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1921         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1922           // Check for reassembly timeout (fragments arriving slowly)
1923           // This is NOT a connection failure - safe to retry
1924           if ((err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) && err_ctx.context_message &&
1925               strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "reassembly timeout")) {
1926             // Fragments are arriving slowly - this is normal, retry without disconnecting
1927             log_dev_every(100000, "Client %s: fragment reassembly timeout, retrying in 10ms", client->client_id);
1928             platform_sleep_ms(10); // Sleep 10ms to allow fragments to arrive
1929             continue;              // Retry without disconnecting
1930           }
1931 
1932           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1933             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1934             break;
1935           }
1936         }
1937         log_warn("Receive failed for WebRTC client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1938                  asciichat_error_string(recv_result));
1939         break;
1940       }
1941 
1942       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: transport->recv() returned result=%d, packet_len=%zu, allocated_buffer=%p",
1943                client->client_id, recv_result, packet_len, allocated_buffer);
1944 
1945       // Validate received packet before queueing
1946       if (packet_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1947         log_warn("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Received packet too small (%zu < %zu), dropping", client->client_id, packet_len,
1948                  sizeof(packet_header_t));
1949         if (allocated_buffer) {
1950           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1951         }
1952         continue;
1953       }
1954 
1955       // Queue the received packet for async dispatch
1956       // This prevents the receive thread from blocking on dispatch
1957       log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Queuing %zu byte packet for async dispatch", client->client_id, packet_len);
1958 
1959       // Extract packet type from the header to preserve it when queueing
1960       const packet_header_t *pkt_header = (const packet_header_t *)allocated_buffer;
1961       packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(pkt_header->type);
1962       uint32_t payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(pkt_header->length);
1963 
1964       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Packet header: type=%d, payload_len=%u, total_len=%zu", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1965                payload_len, packet_len);
1966 
1967       // Build a complete packet to queue (header + payload)
1968       // The entire buffer (allocated_buffer) contains the full packet
1969       // copy_data=false because we want to transfer ownership to the queue
1970       uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
1971       int enqueue_result = packet_queue_enqueue(client->received_packet_queue, pkt_type, allocated_buffer, packet_len,
1972                                                 client_id_hash, false);
1973 
1974       if (enqueue_result < 0) {
1975         log_error("🔴 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Failed to queue received packet (queue full?) - DROPPING FRAME",
1976                   client->client_id);
1977         if (allocated_buffer) {
1978           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1979         }
1980       } else {
1981         log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Successfully queued packet (type=%d, len=%zu)", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1982                  packet_len);
1983         // Signal dispatch thread to wake up and process the newly queued packet
1984         cond_signal(&client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1985       }
1986     }
1987   }
1988 
1989   // Mark client as inactive and stop all threads
1990   // Must stop render threads when client disconnects.
1991   // OPTIMIZED: Use atomic operations for thread control flags (lock-free)
1992   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
1993   log_debug("Setting active=false in receive_thread_fn (client_id=%s, exiting receive loop)", client_id_snapshot);
1994   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1995   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
1996   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
1997   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
1998 
1999   // Call remove_client() to trigger cleanup
2000   // Safe to call from receive thread now: remove_client() detects self-join via thread IDs
2001   // and skips the receive thread join when called from the receive thread itself
2002   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s calling remove_client() for cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2003   server_context_t *server_ctx = (server_context_t *)client->server_ctx;
2004   if (server_ctx) {
2005     if (remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot) != 0) {
2006       log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from receive thread cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2007     }
2008   } else {
2009     log_error("Receive thread for client %s: server_ctx is NULL, cannot call remove_client()", client_id_snapshot);
2010   }
2011 
2012   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s terminated", client_id_snapshot);
2013 
2014   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
2015   asciichat_errno_destroy();
2016 
2017   return NULL;
2018 }






References acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(),
    client_info::active, asciichat_errno_destroy(),

    client_info::client_id, asciichat_error_context_t::code,
    cond_signal(), asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,

    HAS_ERRNO, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,

    NET_TO_HOST_U16, NET_TO_HOST_U32,
    packet_queue_enqueue(), platform_sleep_ms(),

    client_info::receive_thread_id,
    client_info::received_packet_queue,

void
  * client_send_thread_func (void * arg)


Client packet send thread.
