  src/server/client.c.


2021                                          {
2022   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
2023 
2024   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.
2025   // This prevents crashes if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct
2026   // while the thread was still starting at RtlUserThreadStart.
2027   if (!client) {
2028     log_error("Invalid client info in send thread (NULL pointer)");
2029     return NULL;
2030   }
2031 
2032   // Check if client_id is empty (client struct has been zeroed by remove_client)
2033   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
2034   if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
2035     log_debug_every(100 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2036                     "Send thread: client_id is empty, client struct may have been zeroed, exiting");
2037     return NULL;
2038   }
2039 
2040   // Additional validation: check socket OR transport is valid
2041   // For TCP clients: socket is valid
2042   // For WebRTC clients: socket is INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE but transport is valid
2043   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2044   bool has_socket = (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE);
2045   bool has_transport = (client->transport != NULL);
2046   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2047 
2048   log_info("SEND_THREAD_VALIDATION: client_id=%s socket_valid=%d transport_valid=%d transport_ptr=%p",
2049            client->client_id, has_socket, has_transport, (void *)client->transport);
2050 
2051   if (!has_socket && !has_transport) {
2052     log_error("Invalid client connection in send thread (no socket or transport)");
2053     return NULL;
2054   }
2055 
2056   log_info("Started send thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
2057 
2058   // Mark thread as running
2059   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, true);
2060 
2061   log_info("SEND_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s active=%d shutting_down=%d running=%d", client->client_id,
2062            atomic_load_bool(&client->active), atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down),
2063            atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running));
2064 
2065   // Track timing for video frame sends
2066   uint64_t last_video_send_time = 0;
2067   const uint64_t video_send_interval_us = 16666; // 60fps = ~16.67ms
2068 
2069   // High-frequency audio loop - separate from video frame loop
2070   // to ensure audio packets are sent immediately, not rate-limited by video
2071 #define MAX_AUDIO_BATCH 8
2072   int loop_iteration_count = 0;
2073   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) &&
2074          atomic_load_bool(&client->active) && atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running)) {
2075     loop_iteration_count++;
2076     bool sent_something = false;
2077     uint64_t loop_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2078     log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] START: client=%s", loop_iteration_count, client->client_id);
2079 
2080     // PRIORITY: Drain all queued audio packets before video
2081     // Audio must not be rate-limited by video frame sending (16.67ms)
2082     queued_packet_t *audio_packets[MAX_AUDIO_BATCH];
2083     int audio_packet_count = 0;
2084 
2085     if (client->audio_queue) {
2086       // Try to dequeue multiple audio packets
2087       for (int i = 0; i < MAX_AUDIO_BATCH; i++) {
2088         audio_packets[i] = packet_queue_try_dequeue(client->audio_queue);
2089         if (audio_packets[i]) {
2090           audio_packet_count++;
2091         } else {
2092           break; // No more packets available
2093         }
2094       }
2095       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2096         log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_AUDIO: client=%s dequeued=%d packets", client->client_id,
2097                       audio_packet_count);
2098       }
2099     } else {
2100       log_warn("Send thread: audio_queue is NULL for client %s", client->client_id);
2101     }
2102 
2103     // Send batched audio if we have packets
2104     if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2105       // Protect crypto field access with mutex
2106       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2107       bool crypto_ready = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
2108                           crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
2109       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2110 
2111       // Audio packets cannot be sent until crypto handshake completes (protocol requirement)
2112       if (!crypto_ready) {
2113         log_dev_every(1000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SKIP_AUDIO: client=%s handshake not ready, dropping %d audio packets",
2114                       client->client_id, audio_packet_count);
2115         // Drop audio packets until handshake completes
2116         for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2117           packet_queue_free_packet(audio_packets[i]);
2118         }
2119         audio_packet_count = 0;
2120       }
2121 
2122       asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
2123 
2124       // Only send audio if packets survived crypto check (count > 0 after potential drop)
2125       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2126         if (audio_packet_count == 1) {
2127           // Single packet - send directly for low latency using ACIP transport
2128           packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(audio_packets[0]->header.type);
2129 
2130           // Get transport reference while holding mutex briefly (prevents deadlock on TCP buffer full)
2131           mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2132           if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2133             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2134             log_warn("BREAK_AUDIO_SINGLE: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2135                      atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2136             break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2137           }
2138           acip_transport_t *transport = client->transport;
2139           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2140 
2141           // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2142           uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
2143           result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, pkt_type, audio_packets[0]->data, audio_packets[0]->data_len,
2144                                              client_id_hash);
2145           if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2146             log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL: client=%s, len=%zu, result=%d", client->client_id, audio_packets[0]->data_len,
2147                       result);
2148           }
2149         } else {
2150           // Multiple packets - batch them together and send via transport (works for all client types)
2151           packet_type_t first_pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(audio_packets[0]->header.type);
2152 
2153           // Get transport reference
2154           mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2155           if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2156             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2157             log_warn("BREAK_AUDIO_BATCH: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2158                      atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2159             result = ERROR_NETWORK;
2160           } else {
2161             acip_transport_t *transport = client->transport;
2162             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2163 
2164             if (first_pkt_type == PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH) {
2165               // Opus packets - batch and send via transport
2166               size_t total_opus_size = 0;
2167               for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2168                 total_opus_size += audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2169               }
2170 
2171               uint8_t *batched_opus = SAFE_MALLOC(total_opus_size, uint8_t *);
2172               uint16_t *frame_sizes = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)audio_packet_count * sizeof(uint16_t), uint16_t *);
2173 
2174               if (batched_opus && frame_sizes) {
2175                 size_t offset = 0;
2176                 for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2177                   frame_sizes[i] = (uint16_t)audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2178                   memcpy(batched_opus + offset, audio_packets[i]->data, audio_packets[i]->data_len);
2179                   offset += audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2180                 }
2181                 result = acip_send_audio_opus_batch(transport, batched_opus, total_opus_size, frame_sizes,
2182                                                     (uint32_t)audio_packet_count, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE, 20);
2183                 if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2184                   log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL (opus batch): client=%u, frames=%d, total_size=%zu, result=%d",
2185                             client->client_id, audio_packet_count, total_opus_size, result);
2186                 }
2187               } else {
2188                 log_error("Failed to allocate buffer for Opus batch");
2189                 result = ERROR_MEMORY;
2190               }
2191               SAFE_FREE(batched_opus);
2192               SAFE_FREE(frame_sizes);
2193             } else {
2194               // Raw float audio - batch and send via transport
2195               size_t total_samples = 0;
2196               for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2197                 total_samples += audio_packets[i]->data_len / sizeof(float);
2198               }
2199 
2200               float *batched_audio = SAFE_MALLOC(total_samples * sizeof(float), float *);
2201               if (batched_audio) {
2202                 size_t offset = 0;
2203                 for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2204                   size_t packet_samples = audio_packets[i]->data_len / sizeof(float);
2205                   memcpy(batched_audio + offset, audio_packets[i]->data, audio_packets[i]->data_len);
2206                   offset += packet_samples;
2207                 }
2208                 result = acip_send_audio_batch(transport, batched_audio, (uint32_t)total_samples, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
2209                 if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2210                   log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL (raw batch): client=%u, packets=%d, samples=%zu, result=%d",
2211                             client->client_id, audio_packet_count, total_samples, result);
2212                 }
2213               } else {
2214                 log_error("Failed to allocate buffer for audio batch");
2215                 result = ERROR_MEMORY;
2216               }
2217               SAFE_FREE(batched_audio);
2218             }
2219           }
2220         }
2221       } // End of if (audio_packet_count > 0)
2222 
2223       // Free all audio packets
2224       for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2225         packet_queue_free_packet(audio_packets[i]);
2226       }
2227 
2228       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2229         if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2230           log_error("Failed to send audio to client %s: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(result));
2231         }
2232         // Network errors corrupt the TCP stream - must disconnect immediately
2233         if (result == ERROR_NETWORK) {
2234           log_error("CLOSING_CLIENT_ON_NETWORK_ERROR: client_id=%s due to audio send failure (corrupted stream)",
2235                     client->client_id);
2236           break; // Exit send loop to trigger cleanup
2237         }
2238         log_warn("SKIP_AUDIO_ERROR: client_id=%s result=%d (continuing to send video)", client->client_id, result);
2239         // Continue sending video for non-network errors (audio is optional for browser clients)
2240       }
2241 
2242       sent_something = true;
2243       uint64_t audio_done_ns = time_get_ns();
2244       char audio_elapsed_str[32];
2245       time_pretty(audio_done_ns - loop_start_ns, -1, audio_elapsed_str, sizeof(audio_elapsed_str));
2246       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] AUDIO_SENT: took %s", loop_iteration_count,
2247                      audio_elapsed_str);
2248 
2249       // Small sleep to let more audio packets queue (helps batching efficiency)
2250       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2251         platform_sleep_us(100); // 0.1ms - minimal delay
2252       }
2253     } else {
2254       // No audio packets - brief sleep to avoid busy-looping, then check for other tasks
2255       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] NO_AUDIO: sleeping 1ms", loop_iteration_count);
2256       platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms - enough for audio render thread to queue more packets
2257 
2258       // Check if session rekeying should be triggered
2259       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2260       bool should_rekey = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
2261                           crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx) &&
2262                           crypto_handshake_should_rekey(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
2263       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2264 
2265       if (should_rekey) {
2266         log_debug("Rekey threshold reached for client %s, initiating session rekey", client->client_id);
2267         mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2268         // Get socket reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2269         mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2270         if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2271           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2272           mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2273           log_warn("BREAK_REKEY: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d socket=%d", client->client_id,
2274                    atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (int)client->socket);
2275           break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2276         }
2277         socket_t rekey_socket = client->socket;
2278         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2279 
2280         // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the send_mutex (client_state_mutex still held for crypto state)
2281         asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_rekey_request(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, rekey_socket);
2282         mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2283 
2284         if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2285           log_error("Failed to send REKEY_REQUEST to client %s: %d", client->client_id, result);
2286         } else {
2287           log_debug("Sent REKEY_REQUEST to client %s", client->client_id);
2288           // Notify client that session rekeying has been initiated (old keys still active)
2289           log_info_client(client, "Session rekey initiated - rotating encryption keys");
2290         }
2291       }
2292     }
2293 
2294     // Always consume frames from the buffer to prevent accumulation
2295     // Rate-limit the actual sending, but always mark frames as consumed
2296     uint64_t video_check_ns = time_get_ns();
2297     if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
2298       // Buffer has been destroyed (client is shutting down).
2299       // Exit cleanly instead of looping forever trying to access freed memory.
2300       log_warn("⚠️  Send thread exiting: outgoing_video_buffer is NULL for client %s (client shutting down?)",
2301                client->client_id);
2302       break;
2303     }
2304 
2305     // Get latest frame from double buffer (lock-free operation)
2306     // This marks the frame as consumed even if we don't send it yet
2307     const video_frame_t *frame = video_frame_get_latest(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
2308     uint64_t frame_get_ns = time_get_ns();
2309     char frame_get_elapsed_str[32];
2310     time_pretty(frame_get_ns - video_check_ns, -1, frame_get_elapsed_str, sizeof(frame_get_elapsed_str));
2311     log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] VIDEO_GET_FRAME: took %s, frame=%p", loop_iteration_count,
2312                    frame_get_elapsed_str, (void *)frame);
2313     log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Send thread: video_frame_get_latest returned %p for client %s", (void *)frame,
2314                   client->client_id);
2315 
2316     // Check if get_latest failed (buffer might have been destroyed)
2317     if (!frame) {
2318       log_warn("⚠️  Send thread exiting: video_frame_get_latest returned NULL for client %s (buffer destroyed?)",
2319                client->client_id);
2320       break; // Exit thread if buffer is invalid
2321     }
2322 
2323     // Check if it's time to send a video frame (60fps rate limiting)
2324     // Only rate-limit the SEND operation, not frame consumption
2325     uint64_t current_time_ns = time_get_ns();
2326     uint64_t current_time_us = time_ns_to_us(current_time_ns);
2327     uint64_t time_since_last_send_us = current_time_us - last_video_send_time;
2328     log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2329                   "Send thread timing check: time_since_last=%llu us, interval=%llu us, should_send=%d",
2330                   (unsigned long long)time_since_last_send_us, (unsigned long long)video_send_interval_us,
2331                   (time_since_last_send_us >= video_send_interval_us));
2332 
2333     if (current_time_us - last_video_send_time >= video_send_interval_us) {
2334       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✓ SEND_TIME_READY: client_id=%s time_since=%llu interval=%llu",
2335                      client->client_id, (unsigned long long)time_since_last_send_us,
2336                      (unsigned long long)video_send_interval_us);
2337       uint64_t frame_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2338 
2339       // GRID LAYOUT CHANGE: Check if render thread has buffered a frame with different source count
2340       // If so, send CLEAR_CONSOLE before sending the new frame
2341       int rendered_sources = atomic_load_bool(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources);
2342       int sent_sources = atomic_load_bool(&client->last_sent_grid_sources);
2343 
2344       if (rendered_sources != sent_sources && rendered_sources > 0) {
2345         // Grid layout changed! Send CLEAR_CONSOLE before next frame using ACIP transport
2346         // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2347         mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2348         if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2349           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2350           log_warn("BREAK_CLEAR_CONSOLE: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2351                    atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2352           break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2353         }
2354         acip_transport_t *clear_transport = client->transport;
2355         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2356 
2357         // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2358         acip_send_clear_console(clear_transport);
2359         log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_FAST, "Client %s: Sent CLEAR_CONSOLE (grid changed %d → %d sources)",
2360                         client->client_id, sent_sources, rendered_sources);
2361         atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, rendered_sources);
2362         sent_something = true;
2363       }
2364 
2365       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2366                      "🎯 FRAME_VALIDATION: client_id=%s frame=%p, frame->data=%p, frame->size=%zu", client->client_id,
2367                      (void *)frame, (void *)frame->data, frame->size);
2368 
2369       if (!frame->data) {
2370         log_info("❌ SKIP_NO_DATA: client_id=%s frame=%p data=%p", client->client_id, (void *)frame,
2371                  (void *)frame->data);
2372         continue;
2373       }
2374       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ FRAME_DATA_OK: client_id=%s data=%p", client->client_id,
2375                      (void *)frame->data);
2376 
2377       if (frame->data && frame->size == 0) {
2378         log_info("❌ SKIP_ZERO_SIZE: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame->size);
2379         platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms sleep
2380         continue;
2381       }
2382       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ FRAME_SIZE_OK: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame->size);
2383 
2384       // Snapshot frame metadata (safe with double-buffer system)
2385       const char *frame_data = (const char *)frame->data; // Pointer snapshot - data is stable in front buffer
2386       size_t frame_size = frame->size;                    // Size snapshot - prevent race condition with render thread
2387       uint32_t width = client->width;
2388       uint32_t height = client->height;
2389       uint64_t step1_ns = time_get_ns();
2390       uint64_t step2_ns = time_get_ns();
2391       uint64_t step3_ns = time_get_ns();
2392       uint64_t step4_ns = time_get_ns();
2393 
2394       // Check if crypto handshake is complete before sending (prevents sending to unauthenticated clients)
2395       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2396       bool crypto_ready = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) ||
2397                           (client->crypto_initialized && crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
2398       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2399 
2400       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2401                      "🔐 CRYPTO_CHECK: client_id=%s crypto_ready=%d crypto_initialized=%d no_encrypt=%d",
2402                      client->client_id, crypto_ready, client->crypto_initialized, GET_OPTION(no_encrypt));
2403 
2404       if (!crypto_ready) {
2405         log_info("⚠️  SKIP_SEND_CRYPTO: client_id=%s crypto_initialized=%d no_encrypt=%d", client->client_id,
2406                  client->crypto_initialized, GET_OPTION(no_encrypt));
2407         continue; // Skip this frame, will try again on next loop iteration
2408       }
2409       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ CRYPTO_READY: client_id=%s about to send frame", client->client_id);
2410 
2411       // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2412       // ACIP transport handles header building, CRC32, encryption internally
2413       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2414                     "Send thread: About to send frame to client %s (width=%u, height=%u, size=%zu, data=%p)",
2415                     client->client_id, width, height, frame_size, (void *)frame_data);
2416 
2417       // Log first 32 bytes of frame data to verify we can access it
2418       if (frame_data && frame_size > 0) {
2419         log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2420                        "FRAME_DATA_HEX: client=%u first_bytes=[%02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x "
2421                        "%02x %02x %02x %02x %02x]",
2422                        client->client_id, ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[0], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[1],
2423                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[2], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[3], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[4],
2424                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[5], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[6], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[7],
2425                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[8], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[9], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[10],
2426                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[11], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[12], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[13],
2427                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[14], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[15]);
2428       }
2429 
2430       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2431       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2432         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2433         log_warn("BREAK_FRAME_SEND: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p loop_iter=%d", client->client_id,
2434                  atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport, loop_iteration_count);
2435         break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2436       }
2437       acip_transport_t *frame_transport = client->transport;
2438       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2439 
2440       // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2441       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_ASCII_FRAME: client_id=%s size=%zu width=%u height=%u",
2442                     client->client_id, frame_size, width, height);
2443       uint64_t send_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2444       log_dev("[SEND_LOOP_%d] FRAME_SEND_START: size=%zu", loop_iteration_count, frame_size);
2445       asciichat_error_t send_result =
2446           acip_send_ascii_frame(frame_transport, frame_data, frame_size, width, height, client->client_id);
2447       uint64_t send_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2448       char send_elapsed_str[32];
2449       time_pretty(send_end_ns - send_start_ns, -1, send_elapsed_str, sizeof(send_elapsed_str));
2450       log_dev("[SEND_LOOP_%d] FRAME_SEND_END: took %s, result=%d", loop_iteration_count, send_elapsed_str, send_result);
2451       uint64_t step5_ns = time_get_ns();
2452 
2453       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2454         if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2455           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send video frame to client %s: %s", client->client_id,
2456                     asciichat_error_string(send_result));
2457         }
2458         log_error("SEND_FRAME_FAILED: client_id=%s result=%d message=%s loop_iter=%d", client->client_id, send_result,
2459                   asciichat_error_string(send_result), loop_iteration_count);
2460         log_warn("BREAK_SEND_ERROR: client_id=%s (frame send failed)", client->client_id);
2461         break;
2462       }
2463 
2464       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_FRAME_SUCCESS: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame_size);
2465 
2466       // Increment frame counter and log
2467       unsigned long frame_count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&client->frames_sent_count, 1) + 1;
2468       log_info("🎬 FRAME_SENT: client_id=%s frame_num=%lu size=%zu", client->client_id, frame_count, frame_size);
2469 
2470       sent_something = true;
2471       last_video_send_time = current_time_us;
2472 
2473       uint64_t frame_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2474       uint64_t frame_time_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(frame_start_ns, frame_end_ns));
2475       if (frame_time_us > 15 * US_PER_MS_INT) { // Log if sending a frame takes > 15ms (encryption adds ~5-6ms)
2476         uint64_t step1_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(frame_start_ns, step1_ns));
2477         uint64_t step2_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step1_ns, step2_ns));
2478         uint64_t step3_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step2_ns, step3_ns));
2479         uint64_t step4_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step3_ns, step4_ns));
2480         uint64_t step5_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step4_ns, step5_ns));
2481         log_warn_every(
2482             LOG_RATE_DEFAULT,
2483             "SEND_THREAD: Frame send took %.2fms for client %s | Snapshot: %.2fms | Memcpy: %.2fms | CRC32: %.2fms | "
2484             "Header: %.2fms | send_packet_secure: %.2fms",
2485             frame_time_us / 1000.0, client->client_id, step1_us / 1000.0, step2_us / 1000.0, step3_us / 1000.0,
2486             step4_us / 1000.0, step5_us / 1000.0);
2487       }
2488     }
2489 
2490     // If we didn't send anything, sleep briefly to prevent busy waiting
2491     if (!sent_something) {
2492       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] IDLE_SLEEP: nothing sent", loop_iteration_count);
2493       platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms sleep
2494     }
2495     uint64_t loop_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2496     uint64_t loop_elapsed_ns = loop_end_ns - loop_start_ns;
2497     if (loop_elapsed_ns > 10 * NS_PER_MS) {
2498       char loop_elapsed_str[32];
2499       time_pretty(loop_elapsed_ns, -1, loop_elapsed_str, sizeof(loop_elapsed_str));
2500       log_warn("[SEND_LOOP_%d] SLOW_ITERATION: took %s (client=%u)", loop_iteration_count, loop_elapsed_str,
2501                client->client_id);
2502     }
2503   }
2504 
2505   // Log why the send thread exited
2506   log_warn("Send thread exit conditions - client_id=%s g_should_exit=%d shutting_down=%d active=%d "
2507            "send_thread_running=%d",
2508            client->client_id, atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit), atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down),
2509            atomic_load_bool(&client->active), atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running));
2510 
2511   // Mark thread as stopped
2512   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2513   log_debug("Send thread for client %s terminated", client->client_id);
2514 
2515   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
2516   asciichat_errno_destroy();
2517 
2518   return NULL;
2519 }






References acip_send_ascii_frame(),
    acip_send_audio_batch(), acip_send_audio_opus_batch(),
    acip_send_clear_console(), client_info::active,
    asciichat_errno_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_fetch_add_u64(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), atomic_store_u64(),
    client_info::audio_queue, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE,
    client_info::client_id, client_info::client_state_mutex,
    client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), crypto_handshake_rekey_request(),
    crypto_handshake_should_rekey(),
    client_info::crypto_initialized, queued_packet_t::data_len,
    client_info::display_name, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    frame, client_info::frames_sent_count, g_should_exit,
    GET_OPTION, client_info::height, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    client_info::last_rendered_grid_sources,
    client_info::last_sent_grid_sources, log_debug,
    log_debug_every, log_dev, log_dev_every,
    log_error, log_info, log_info_client,

client_info_t * find_client_by_id (const char *
  client_id)


Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. This is the
    primary method for locating clients throughout the server. It uses a hash
    table for constant-time lookups regardless of client count, making it
    suitable for high-performance operations like rendering and stats.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(1) average case, O(n) worst case (hash collision)
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Hash table is internally thread-safe for lookups
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Called by render threads to find target clients for frame generation
    • 
  
  • Used by protocol handlers to locate clients for packet processing
    • 
  
  • Stats collection for per-client performance monitoring
    • 



Parameters


client_id Unique identifier for the client (0 is
  invalid)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found or invalid ID


Note


Does not require external locking - hash table provides
  thread safety



Returns direct pointer to client struct - caller should use
    snapshot pattern




Definition at line 386 of file
  src/server/client.c.


386                                                         {
387   if (!client_id || client_id[0] == '\0') {
388     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid client ID");
389     return NULL;
390   }
391 
392   // Protect uthash lookup with read lock to prevent concurrent access issues
393   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
394 
395   client_info_t *result = NULL;
396   HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, result);
397 
398   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
399 
400   if (!result) {
401     log_warn("Client not found for ID %s", client_id);
402   }
403 
404   return result;
405 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, g_client_manager,
    g_client_manager_rwlock, log_warn, rwlock_rdlock,
    rwlock_rdunlock, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(),
    crypto_server_cleanup_client(),
    crypto_server_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_server_encrypt_packet(), crypto_server_get_context(),
    crypto_server_is_ready(), and
  start_webrtc_client_threads().






client_info_t * find_client_by_socket (socket_t
  socket)


Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. This
    function provides socket-based client lookup, primarily used during
    connection establishment before client IDs are assigned. Less efficient than
    find_client_by_id() but necessary for socket-based operations.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(n) where n = number of active clients
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Internally acquires read lock on
    g_client_manager_rwlock
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Connection establishment during add_client() processing
    • 
  
  • Socket error handling and cleanup operations
    • 
  
  • Debugging and diagnostic functions
    • 



Parameters


socket Platform-abstracted socket descriptor to
  search for


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found


Note


Only searches active clients (avoids returning stale
  entries)



Caller should use snapshot pattern when accessing returned client
    data




Definition at line 430 of file
  src/server/client.c.


430                                                       {
431   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
432 
433   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
434     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].socket == socket && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
435       client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
436       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
437       return client;
438     }
439   }
440 
441   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
442   return NULL;
443 }

client Client info structure


type Packet type


data Packet data


len Packet length

3742     log_debug("CLIENT: client_id=%s, data=%p, len=%zu", client->client_id, data, len);
3743   }
3744 
3745   // Rate limiting: Check and record packet-specific rate limits
3746   if (g_rate_limiter) {
3747     if (!check_and_record_packet_rate_limit(g_rate_limiter, client->client_ip, client->socket, type)) {
3748       // Rate limit exceeded - error response already sent by utility function
3749       return;
3750     }
3751   }
3752 
3753   // O(1) dispatch via hash table lookup
3754   int idx = client_dispatch_hash_lookup(g_client_dispatch_hash, type);
3755   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3756     log_error("DISPATCH_LOOKUP: type=%d, idx=%d (len=%zu)", type, idx, len);
3757   }
3758   if (idx < 0) {
3759     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Unknown packet type: %d (len=%zu)", type, len);
3760     return;
3761   }
3762 
3763   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3764     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, calling handler[%d]...", type, idx);
3765   }
3766   g_client_dispatch_handlers[idx](client, data, len);
3767   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3768     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, handler returned", type);
3769   }
3770 }

int
  process_encrypted_packet (client_info_t * client, packet_type_t
  * type, void ** data, size_t * len, uint32_t * sender_id)


Process an encrypted packet from a client




Parameters


client Client info structure


type Pointer to packet type (will be updated with decrypted type)


data Pointer to packet data (will be updated with decrypted data)


len Pointer to packet length (will be updated with decrypted length)


sender_id Pointer to sender ID (will be updated with decrypted sender
  ID)


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error


Definition at line 2804 of file
  src/server/client.c.


2805                                                   {
2806   if (!crypto_server_is_ready(client->client_id)) {
2807     log_error("Received encrypted packet but crypto not ready for client %s", client->client_id);
2808     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2809     *data = NULL;
2810     return -1;
2811   }
2812 
2813   // Store original allocation size before it gets modified
2814   size_t original_alloc_size = *len;
2815   void *decrypted_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, original_alloc_size);
2816   size_t decrypted_len;
2817   int decrypt_result = crypto_server_decrypt_packet(client->client_id, (const uint8_t *)*data, *len,
2818                                                     (uint8_t *)decrypted_data, original_alloc_size, &decrypted_len);
2819 
2820   if (decrypt_result != 0) {
2821     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to process encrypted packet from client %s (result=%d)", client->client_id,
2822               decrypt_result);
2823     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2824     buffer_pool_free(NULL, decrypted_data, original_alloc_size);
2825     *data = NULL;
2826     return -1;
2827   }
2828 
2829   // Replace encrypted data with decrypted data
2830   // Use original allocation size for freeing the encrypted buffer
2831   buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2832 
2833   *data = decrypted_data;
2834   *len = decrypted_len;
2835 
2836   // Now process the decrypted packet by parsing its header
2837   if (*len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
2838     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Decrypted packet too small for header from client %s", client->client_id);
2839     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2840     *data = NULL;
2841     return -1;
2842   }
2843 
2844   packet_header_t *header = (packet_header_t *)*data;
2845   *type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
2846   *sender_id = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->client_id);
2847 
2848   // Adjust data pointer to skip header
2849   *data = (uint8_t *)*data + sizeof(packet_header_t);
2850   *len -= sizeof(packet_header_t);
2851 
2852   return 0;
2853 }

int
  remove_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char *
  client_id)


Definition at line 1323 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1323                                                                        {
1324   if (!server_ctx) {
1325     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Cannot remove client %s: NULL server_ctx", client_id);
1326     return -1;
1327   }
1328 
1329   // Phase 1: Mark client inactive and prepare for cleanup while holding write lock
1330   client_info_t *target_client = NULL;
1331   char display_name_copy[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
1332   socket_t client_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1333 
1334   log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Attempting to remove client %s", client_id);
1335   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1336 
1337   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1338     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
1339     if (strcmp(client->client_id, client_id) == 0 && client->client_id[0] != '\0') {
1340       // Check if already being removed by another thread
1341       // This prevents double-free and use-after-free crashes during concurrent cleanup
1342       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1343         rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1344         log_debug("Client %s already being removed by another thread, skipping", client_id);
1345         return 0; // Return success - removal is in progress
1346       }
1347       // Mark as shutting down and inactive immediately to stop new operations
1348       log_debug("Setting active=false in remove_client (client_id=%s, socket=%d)", client_id, client->socket);
1349       log_info("Removing client %s (socket=%d) - marking inactive and clearing video flags", client_id, client->socket);
1350       atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
1351       atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1352       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, false);
1353       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, false);
1354       target_client = client;
1355 
1356       // Store display name before clearing
1357       SAFE_STRNCPY(display_name_copy, client->display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN - 1);
1358 
1359       // Save socket for tcp_server_stop_client_threads() before closing
1360       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1361       client_socket = client->socket; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1362       // NOTE: Do NOT call socket_shutdown() here - it sends FIN to peer and breaks the connection
1363       // The threads will exit naturally when they check shutting_down/active flags or hit errors
1364       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1365 
1366       // Shutdown packet queues to unblock send thread
1367       if (client->audio_queue) {
1368         packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
1369       }
1370       // Video now uses double buffer, no queue to shutdown
1371 
1372       break;
1373     }
1374   }
1375 
1376   // If client not found, unlock and return
1377   if (!target_client) {
1378     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1379     log_warn("Cannot remove client %s: not found", client_id);
1380     return -1;
1381   }
1382 
1383   // Unregister client from session_host (for discovery mode support)
1384   // NOTE: Client may not be registered if crypto handshake failed before session_host registration
1385   if (server_ctx->session_host && target_client->session_client_id != 0) {
1386     asciichat_error_t session_result =
1387         session_host_remove_client(server_ctx->session_host, target_client->session_client_id);
1388     if (session_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1389       // ERROR_NOT_FOUND (91) is expected if client failed crypto before being registered with session_host
1390       if (session_result == ERROR_NOT_FOUND) {
1391         log_debug("Client %s not found in session_host (likely failed crypto before registration)", client_id);
1392       } else {
1393         log_warn("Failed to unregister client %s from session_host: %s", client_id,
1394                  asciichat_error_string(session_result));
1395       }
1396     } else {
1397       log_debug("Client %s unregistered from session_host", client_id);
1398     }
1399   }
1400 
1401   // Release write lock before joining threads.
1402   // This prevents deadlock with render threads that need read locks.
1403   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1404 
1405   // Phase 2: Stop all client threads
1406   // For TCP clients: use tcp_server thread pool management
1407   // For WebRTC clients: manually join threads (no socket-based thread pool)
1408   // Use is_tcp_client flag, not socket value - socket may already be INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE
1409   // even for TCP clients if it was closed earlier during cleanup.
1410   log_debug("Stopping all threads for client %s (socket %d, is_tcp=%d)", client_id, client_socket,
1411             target_client ? target_client->is_tcp_client : -1);
1412 
1413   if (target_client && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1414     // TCP client: use tcp_server thread pool
1415     // This joins threads in stop_id order: receive(1), render(2), send(3)
1416     // Use saved client_socket for lookup (tcp_server needs original socket as key)
1417     if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1418       asciichat_error_t stop_result = tcp_server_stop_client_threads(server_ctx->tcp_server, client_socket);
1419       if (stop_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1420         log_warn("Failed to stop threads for TCP client %s: error %d", client_id, stop_result);
1421         // Continue with cleanup even if thread stopping failed
1422       }
1423     } else {
1424       log_debug("TCP client %s socket already closed, threads should have already exited", client_id);
1425     }
1426   } else if (target_client) {
1427     // WebRTC client: manually join threads
1428     log_debug("Stopping WebRTC client %s threads (receive and send)", client_id);
1429 
1430     // Join receive thread (but skip if called from the receive thread itself to avoid deadlock)
1431     thread_id_t current_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1432     if (asciichat_thread_equal(current_thread_id, target_client->receive_thread_id)) {
1433       log_debug("remove_client() called from receive thread for client %s, skipping self-join", client_id);
1434     } else {
1435       void *recv_result = NULL;
1436       asciichat_error_t recv_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->receive_thread, &recv_result);
1437       if (recv_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1438         log_warn("Failed to join receive thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, recv_join_result);
1439       } else {
1440         log_debug("Joined receive thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1441       }
1442     }
1443 
1444     // Join dispatch thread (BEFORE destroying packet queues)
1445     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->dispatch_thread)) {
1446       atomic_store_bool(&target_client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
1447       void *dispatch_result = NULL;
1448       asciichat_error_t dispatch_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->dispatch_thread, &dispatch_result);
1449       if (dispatch_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1450         log_warn("Failed to join dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, dispatch_join_result);
1451       } else {
1452         log_debug("Joined dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1453       }
1454     }
1455 
1456     // Join send thread
1457     void *send_result = NULL;
1458     asciichat_error_t send_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->send_thread, &send_result);
1459     if (send_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1460       log_warn("Failed to join send thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, send_join_result);
1461     } else {
1462       log_debug("Joined send thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1463     }
1464     // Note: Render threads still need to be stopped - they're created the same way for both TCP and WebRTC
1465     // For now, render threads are expected to exit when they check g_should_exit and client->active
1466   }
1467 
1468   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
1469   // For WebSocket clients: LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already closed and destroyed the transport
1470   // Trying to destroy it again causes heap-use-after-free since LWS callbacks might still fire
1471   // For TCP clients: transport is ours to clean up
1472   if (target_client && target_client->transport && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1473     acip_transport_destroy(target_client->transport);
1474     target_client->transport = NULL;
1475     log_debug("Destroyed ACIP transport for TCP client %s", client_id);
1476   } else if (target_client && target_client->transport && !target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1477     // WebSocket client - just NULL it out, LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already destroyed it
1478     target_client->transport = NULL;
1479     log_debug("Skipped transport destruction for WebSocket client %s (LWS already destroyed)", client_id);
1480   }
1481 
1482   // Now safe to close the socket (threads are stopped)
1483   if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1484     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Closing socket %d for client %s after thread cleanup", client_socket, client_id);
1485     socket_close(client_socket);
1486   }
1487 
1488   // Phase 3: Clean up resources with write lock
1489   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1490 
1491   // Re-validate target_client pointer after reacquiring lock.
1492   // Another thread might have invalidated the pointer while we had the lock released.
1493   if (target_client) {
1494     // Verify client_id still matches and client is still in shutting_down state
1495     bool still_shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&target_client->shutting_down);
1496     if (strcmp(target_client->client_id, client_id) != 0 || !still_shutting_down) {
1497       log_warn("Client %s pointer invalidated during thread cleanup (id=%s, shutting_down=%d)", client_id,
1498                target_client->client_id, still_shutting_down);
1499       rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1500       return 0; // Another thread completed the cleanup
1501     }
1502   }
1503 
1504   // Mark socket as closed in client structure
1505   if (target_client && target_client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1506     mutex_lock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1507     target_client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
1508     mutex_unlock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1509     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Client %s socket set to INVALID", target_client->client_id);
1510   }
1511 
1512   // Use the dedicated cleanup function to ensure all resources are freed
1513   cleanup_client_all_buffers(target_client);
1514 
1515   // Remove from audio mixer
1516   if (g_audio_mixer) {
1517     mixer_remove_source(g_audio_mixer, client_id);
1518 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1519     log_debug("Removed client %s from audio mixer", client_id);
1520 #endif
1521   }
1522 
1523   // Remove from uthash table
1524   // Verify client is actually in the hash table before deleting.
1525   // Another thread might have already removed it.
1526   if (target_client) {
1527     client_info_t *hash_entry = NULL;
1528     HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client->client_id, hash_entry);
1529     if (hash_entry == target_client) {
1530       HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client);
1531       log_debug("Removed client %s from uthash table", client_id);
1532     } else {
1533       log_warn("Client %s already removed from hash table by another thread (found=%p, expected=%p)", client_id,
1534                (void *)hash_entry, (void *)target_client);
1535     }
1536   } else {
1537     log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from hash table (client not found)", client_id);
1538   }
1539 
1540   // Cleanup crypto context for this client
1541   if (target_client->crypto_initialized) {
1542     crypto_handshake_destroy(&target_client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
1543     target_client->crypto_initialized = false;
1544     log_debug("Crypto context cleaned up for client %s", client_id);
1545   }
1546 
1547   // Verify all threads have actually exited before resetting client_id.
1548   // Threads that are still starting (at RtlUserThreadStart) haven't checked client_id yet.
1549   // We must ensure threads are fully joined before zeroing the client struct.
1550   // Use exponential backoff for thread termination verification
1551   int retry_count = 0;
1552   const int max_retries = 5;
1553   while (retry_count < max_retries && (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->send_thread) ||
1554                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->receive_thread) ||
1555                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->video_render_thread) ||
1556                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->audio_render_thread))) {
1557     // Exponential backoff: 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms
1558     uint32_t delay_ms = 10 * (1 << retry_count);
1559     log_warn("Client %s: Some threads still appear initialized (attempt %d/%d), waiting %ums", client_id,
1560              retry_count + 1, max_retries, delay_ms);
1561     platform_sleep_us(delay_ms * 1000);
1562     retry_count++;
1563   }
1564 
1565   if (retry_count == max_retries) {
1566     log_error("Client %s: Threads did not terminate after %d retries, proceeding with cleanup anyway", client_id,
1567               max_retries);
1568   }
1569 
1570   // Only reset client_id to 0 AFTER confirming threads are joined
1571   // This prevents threads that are starting from accessing a zeroed client struct
1572   // Reset client_id to NULL before destroying mutexes to prevent race conditions.
1573   // This ensures worker threads can detect shutdown and exit before the mutex is destroyed.
1574   // If we destroy the mutex first, threads might try to access a destroyed mutex.
1575   target_client->client_id[0] = '\0'; // Clear the client_id string
1576 
1577   // Wait for threads to observe the client_id reset
1578   // Use sufficient delay for memory visibility across all CPU cores
1579   platform_sleep_us(5 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 5ms delay for memory barrier propagation
1580 
1581   // Destroy mutexes and condition variables
1582   // IMPORTANT: Always destroy these even if threads didn't join properly
1583   // to prevent issues when the slot is reused
1584   mutex_destroy(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1585   mutex_destroy(&target_client->send_mutex);
1586   cond_destroy(&target_client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1587 
1588   // Clear client structure
1589   // NOTE: After memset, the mutex handles are zeroed but the OS resources
1590   // have been released by the destroy calls above
1591   memset(target_client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1592 
1593   // Recalculate client count
1594   int remaining_count = 0;
1595   for (int j = 0; j < MAX_CLIENTS; j++) {
1596     if (g_client_manager.clients[j].client_id[0] != '\0') {
1597       remaining_count++;
1598     }
1599   }
1600   g_client_manager.client_count = remaining_count;
1601 
1602   log_debug("Client removed: client_id=%s (%s) removed, remaining clients: %d", client_id, display_name_copy,
1603             remaining_count);
1604 
1605   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1606 
1607   // Broadcast updated state
1608   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1609 
1610   return 0;
1611 }

int
  start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char
  * client_id)


Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization.
    This is called by WebSocket handler after crypto handshake is initialized.
    It ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto
    context exists.




Parameters


server_ctx Server context


client_id Client ID to start threads for


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

2669     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Server context is NULL");
2670     return -1;
2671   }
2672 
2673   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
2674   if (!client) {
2675     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client %s not found", client_id);
2676     return -1;
2677   }
2678 
2679   log_debug("Starting threads for WebRTC client %s...", client_id);
2680   return start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false);
2681 }

void
  stop_client_threads (client_info_t * client)


Definition at line 2683 of file
  src/server/client.c.

2684   if (!client) {
2685     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client is NULL");
2686     return;
2687   }
2688 
2689   // Signal threads to stop
2690   log_debug("Setting active=false in stop_client_threads (client_id=%s)", client->client_id);

2700   }

Variable
  Documentation




client_manager_t g_client_manager


Global client manager singleton - central coordination point.
    Global client manager.




This is the primary data structure for managing all connected
    clients. It serves as the bridge between main.c's connection accept loop and
    the per-client threading architecture.




STRUCTURE COMPONENTS:


    
  
  • clients[]: Array backing storage for client_info_t structs
    • 
  
  • client_hashtable: O(1) lookup table for client_id ->
    client_info_t*
    • 
  
  • client_count: Current number of active clients
    • 
  
  • mutex: Legacy mutex (mostly replaced by rwlock)
    • 
  
  • next_client_id: Monotonic counter for unique client identification
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: Protected by g_client_manager_rwlock for concurrent
    access


Definition at line 333 of file
  src/server/client.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    any_clients_sending_video(),
    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),
    send_server_state_to_client(), server_main(),
    stats_logger_thread(), and update_server_stats().






rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock = {0}


Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager.
    Read-write lock protecting client manager.




This lock enables high-performance concurrent access patterns:


    
  
  • Multiple threads can read client data simultaneously (stats,
    rendering)
    • 
  
  • Only one thread can modify client data at a time (add/remove
    operations)
    • 
  
  • Eliminates contention between read-heavy operations
    • 



USAGE PATTERN:


    
  
  • Read operations: rwlock_rdlock() for client lookups, stats
      gathering
    • 
  
  • Write operations: rwlock_wrlock() for add_client(),
      remove_client()
    • 
  
  • Always acquire THIS lock before per-client mutexes (lock ordering)
    • 



Definition at line 348 of file
  src/server/client.c.


348 {0};

    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),
    server_main(), stats_logger_thread(), and
    update_server_stats().
